fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: California CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to California - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity california, how to transfer CNA license to california, california CNA endorsement application

board.name: California Dept of Public Health
board.mailing_address:
Aide and Technician Certification Section
P.O. Box 997416, MS 3301
Sacramento, CA 95899-7416

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in California.

steps.title: Complete an Approved CNA Training Program
steps.description: Complete a minimum of 160 hours of training in a program approved by the California Department of Public Health (CDPH), including at least 60 hours of classroom instruction and 100 hours of supervised clinical training.

steps.title: Create a BreEZe Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the BreEZe Online Services portal at breeze.ca.gov, if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Submit Your Application Online
steps.description: Log in to your BreEZe account and select the appropriate application for 'Certified Nursing Assistant Certification by Examination'. Complete all required sections and upload necessary documents, such as proof of training completion and the 'Request for Live Scan Service' form (CDPH 283B).

steps.title: Complete Live Scan Fingerprinting
steps.description: Submit your fingerprints electronically via Live Scan for a criminal background check. You must use the 'Request for Live Scan Service' form (CDPH 283B) provided by your training program or available from the CDPH website.

steps.title: Pass the Competency Evaluation Examination
steps.description: Once your application is reviewed and approved, you will be notified of your eligibility to take the competency evaluation examination. Schedule and pass both the written (or oral) and skills portions of the exam through an approved vendor like Pearson VUE.

steps.title: Receive Your CNA Certificate
steps.description: Upon successful completion of all requirements and passing the competency evaluation examination, the CDPH will issue your CNA certificate. You can view and print your certificate directly from your BreEZe account.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does California CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in California. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in California.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to California?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in California while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA license to California.

hero.title: Become a CNA in California
hero.dek: To apply for a Certified Nursing Assistant license in California, you must use the BreEZe Online Services portal for new applications, whether by examination or endorsement.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in California | CDPH Requirements
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to getting your CNA license in California. Learn about the 150-hour training requirement, Live Scan background checks, and the CDPH exam.
target_queries: how to become a cna in california

new_applicant.faq_heading: Common Questions about Getting a CNA in California

sections.label: Basic Requirements
sections.body: To train as a CNA in California, you must be 16 years old, complete Live Scan fingerprinting, and clear a background check.

faq.q: How many hours of training do I need to be a CNA in California?
faq.a: California requires more training than the federal minimum: a total of 150 hours, consisting of 50 hours of theory and 100 hours of clinical training.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to breeze.ca.gov, click 'New User Registration', follow the prompts to create an account, verify your email address, and then log in to access the application system.
